tldr: Nichole, all five of your live ads have been running for 64–71 days with only two distinct creative concepts behind them — and three of those five share near-identical 'NYC vintage + Italian charms' copy. That's well past the 28-day refresh window for a brand your size, and the two-concept count sits below Meta's Andromeda 5-concept floor, which means the algorithm is treating your whole active set as one ad.

benchmark.context: A brand at your scale should be cycling fresh hero creative roughly every 28 days; the typical apparel brand refreshes around 21 days, and top performers are at 14. More urgently, you're running 2 distinct concepts across 5 ads — Meta's Andromeda retrieval engine (live since October 2025) clusters semantically similar creatives and needs at least 5 distinct concepts to avoid suppressing the whole set as one cluster.

ads.body: NYC vintage shopping + custom Italian charm bracelet = elite combo.
drivers: Body copy is verbatim identical to another live video — Andromeda reads these as the same ad and only serves one of them
No headline on this ad — without a headline anchor, the 12-word body is doing all the persuasion work and there's nothing to hook a scroller who pauses
71 days running, well past your 28-day refresh window — the cluster has been audience-saturated for over a month

ads.body: NYC vintage shopping + custom Italian charm bracelet = elite combo.
drivers: Identical body copy to your other charm-bracelet video — running two ads with the same words is functionally one ad to Meta's retrieval engine
No headline at all, so the only thing differentiating this from the duplicate is the video file itself
64 days running with the same single sentence — no hook variation, no offer, no proof

ads.headline: instagram.com
ads.body: Warning: you're about to become a regular 👀 Say hello to your new favorite vintage store. Fresh vintage and secondhand finds for women + men and home decor that sparks joy. Come visit and see for yourself  📍 Upper East Side - 311 E 81st  📍Upper West Side -  2206 Broadway
drivers: Headline reads 'instagram.com' — that's the auto-pulled URL, not a written headline, so the strongest real estate on the ad is wasted
71 days unchanged on a store-visit pitch — the people in your two ZIP code radiuses who were going to walk in have already seen this
Same store-locator structure as your 'We don't gatekeep!' ad, so Andromeda is clustering them together

ads.headline: instagram.com
ads.body: We don't gatekeep! Colorful racks. Fun finds. Women's, men's, accessories, Italian charms, and home decor waiting for you. Come visit and see what everyone's talking about  📍 Upper East Side - 311 E 81st  📍Upper West Side -  2206 Broadway
drivers: Headline is 'instagram.com' — the URL fallback, not a real headline, which Meta's auction reads as a low-effort signal
Structurally a near-duplicate of the 'become a regular' ad: same store-list close, same two pin-emoji addresses, same CTA — one concept dressed two ways
71 days running with no rotation; locals in the Upper East / Upper West catchment have been served this for over two months

ads.body: Finding and sharing the best vintage in NYC. One-of-a-kind pieces you won't find anywhere else. Stop by and discover your next favorite fit.
drivers: No headline — every other store-visit ad you have at least pulls the URL fallback, this one runs naked and loses the headline slot entirely
71 days running on a vintage video that uses the same store-traffic angle as the other foot-traffic ads — it's the third lane of the same concept
Sends to instagram.com/the.niche.shop with a 'Visit Instagram profile' CTA — same destination as all five live ads, so there's zero funnel diversification across the set

hero_concept.hook: The Music Note charm. Found, not designed.
hero_concept.headline: This week's pick: Music Note
hero_concept.primary_text:
Every charm in the shop has a story before it gets to your wrist. The Music Note came in last week — sterling silver, vintage Italian modular, the kind we hunt down so you don't have to.

It clips onto any of our ready-to-wear bracelets in under a minute. One charm, one story, and a small win for circular fashion every time you skip the mall.

DM us on Instagram to claim it before it's gone — these are one-of-one.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Top-down editorial flat-lay of the Music Note sterling silver charm on a soft cream linen surface, with a single vintage Italian charm bracelet curling beside it and a sprig of dried flowers in the corner — warm natural daylight, breathing room top and bottom for ad text.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Your five live ads all sell the store, the neighborhood, or 'NYC vintage + charms' as a vague combo — not a single one features a specific product or a single hero SKU. This concept introduces a product-led lane (charm-of-the-week) that gives Andromeda a genuinely new concept signature and gives scrollers something concrete to want.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a top-down flat-lay on soft cream linen fabric with a single delicate vintage Italian charm bracelet curling loosely beside it and a small sprig of dried lavender or eucalyptus tucked in one corner, with warm natural diffused daylight from a side window casting soft shadows. Editorial vintage-shop aesthetic, hand-curated and intimate, like a single piece pulled from a treasured collection. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact sterling silver / .925 silver finish of the Music Note charm exactly as shown in the reference image, strictly NO gold, brass, yellow, copper, rose-gold, or warm metallic tones, strictly NO color temperature shift toward yellow. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction of the charm: same musical note shape and proportions, same number of openings or jump rings as shown in the reference, same clasp or attachment loop style, same surface finish (polished vs brushed) as shown, same engraved or stamped details if any are visible. Do NOT add additional charms fused to the Music Note, do NOT change the note shape, do NOT add or remove jump rings, do NOT alter the clasp style, do NOT add stones or embellishments not present in the reference. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Two of your charm-bracelet videos hit hard fatigue inside the next week — the rest follow within 15 days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the The Niche Shop refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Nichole, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: We went through every live ad in your Meta Ad Library on May 5, 2026 and scored each on six creative-fatigue signals; we don't have access to your ad account, so all observations are grounded in publicly visible creative, copy, and run-time.
